recommendations: add a minimal MCP smoke-test suite that validates each exposed endpoint imports and executes with tiny synthetic inputs
pin and synchronize dependency constraints across pyproject/setup.py/setup.cfg (plus add a top-level requirements-dev.txt) to reduce resolver drift
add graceful optional-dependency guards with clear error messages for tensorflow/torch/xgboost/lightgbm/shap/pyro-backed endpoints
introduce CI matrix expansion (Python versions + optional extras subsets) and cache wheels to catch compatibility regressions earlier
define and enforce a stable API contract for MCP endpoint names/arguments/return schemas using Pydantic models and versioned schemas
add focused regression tests for high-risk core learners (DR/R/X/meta base classes) around edge cases like treatment imbalance/missing values/multiclass treatment
improve observability by adding structured logging and per-endpoint timing/error metrics in mcp_service
document endpoint-level usage examples and expected I/O in README_MCP.md with a quickstart notebook snippet
add pre-commit hooks for import sorting/type checks (ruff/mypy) and apply consistently to both source and mcp_output
create a release checklist that validates build-from-source
and a representative inference workflow before publishing

success_factors: End-to-end workflow status is success with all planned nodes executed (download, analysis, env, generate, run, review, finalize).
Import-based adapter strategy had high feasibility (0.9) and low intrusiveness risk, enabling fast integration.
Generated MCP plugin started healthy over stdio and passed plugin-level validation.
Repository structure is well-organized and analyzable (pyproject/setup.cfg/setup.py present), supporting automated generation.
failure_reasons: Original project tests are marked failed due to non-execution/empty run metadata (0s runtime, no test files listed), indicating validation gap rather than confirmed code defect.
DeepWiki analysis failed, reducing depth of automated architectural/context enrichment.
